contract.name: v2/disaster/federal_account/loans.md
contract.description: Aggregates disaster and emergency loan spending totals by federal account using required DEF code filters with optional keyword narrowing.

filter.description: Required object containing Disaster Emergency Fund (DEF) code selections; additional keys are accepted but ignored unless explicitly supported.

def_codes.description: List of DEF codes that drive the aggregation.
def_codes.constraints: Required; minItems=1; each entry must be one of ['1','2','3','4','5','6','7','8','9','A','AAA','AAB','AAC','AAD','AAE','AAF','AAG','AAH','AAI','AAJ','AAK','AAL','B','C','D','E','F','G','H','I','J','K','L','M','N','O','P','Q','QQQ','R','S','T','U','V','W','X','Y','Z']; invalid values raise 400.

query.description: Case-insensitive substring match applied to account labels.
query.constraints: Optional; must be non-empty text. Empty strings trigger 422; non-string values trigger 400.

time_period.description: Accepted without validation or observed filtering effect.
time_period.constraints: Optional; malformed or nonsensical values are silently ignored in current behavior.

pagination.description: Optional paging and sorting directives mirrored in metadata only.

limit.description: Requested page size reported in metadata.
limit.constraints: Optional; integer ≥ 1. 0 or negatives return 422; null returns 400. Numeric strings are coerced but still ignored for slicing.

page.description: Requested page index reported in metadata.
page.constraints: Optional; integer ≥ 1. 0 or negatives return 422. Numeric strings are coerced and do not change the record set.

order.constraints: Optional; must be 'asc' or 'desc'. Defaults to 'desc'.

sort.constraints: Optional; allowed values ['id','code','description','award_count','obligation','outlay','face_value_of_loan']. Observed default when omitted sorts by 'code' descending.

spending_type.description: Accepted but currently ignored parameter mirroring documentation examples.
spending_type.constraints: Optional; any string (e.g., 'obligation', 'face_value_of_loan', arbitrary tokens) returns identical aggregates.

totals.description: Overall aggregates across all matched federal accounts.

award_count.description: Total number of awards contributing to the aggregation; zero when no matches.

obligation.description: Sum of obligations for the matched accounts.

outlay.description: Sum of outlays for the matched accounts.

face_value_of_loan.description: Sum of loan face values for the matched accounts.

results.description: List of federal account aggregates ordered per the pagination sort directive.

id.description: Internal identifier for the federal account.

description.description: Human-readable description of the federal account.

award_count.description: Number of awards contributing to this account's totals.

face_value_of_loan.description: Aggregate face value of loans for the account.

children.description: Treasury account rollups belonging to the federal account.

id.description: Internal identifier for the treasury account.

award_count.description: Awards contributing to this treasury account when present.

obligation.description: Obligation total for the treasury account when present.

outlay.description: Outlay total for the treasury account when present.

face_value_of_loan.description: Loan face value total for the treasury account when present.

page_metadata.description: Echoes the requested pagination values without altering the dataset.

total.description: Count of result rows available for the filter.

next.description: Next page number if additional pages are implied, otherwise null.

previous.description: Previous page number if applicable, otherwise null.

hasNext.description: Indicates if more pages are implied by metadata.

hasPrevious.description: Indicates if a prior page is implied by metadata.

results.description: Program Account, United States International Development Finance Corporation

children.description: Program Account, United States International Development Finance Corporation

results.description: Business Loans Program Account, Small Business Administration

quirks: Pagination limit and page values only update metadata; the API still returns the full result set for the provided filter.
'spending_type' accepts any string and does not alter the aggregates.
Malformed or unsupported filter keys (e.g., broken time_period, unknown attributes) are silently ignored without warnings.
Numeric strings for pagination fields are coerced instead of rejected, potentially hiding input errors.
risks: Clients may assume metadata-driven pagination works and inadvertently refetch the complete dataset each request.
Silently ignored or malformed filters can mislead callers into believing results are constrained when they are not.
Large monetary values and award counts require handling beyond 32-bit integer precision.
Extraneous keys accepted inside 'filter' can conceal misconfigured integrations.
gaps: Impact of 'spending_type' remains unverified because all tested values returned identical data.
Unclear which shared filters beyond 'def_codes' are honored; time period inputs had no observable effect.
Unknown whether the service ever enforces actual pagination slicing when result sets exceed observed counts.

probes.notes: pass - minimal request with required def_codes returns data
fail - pagination limit ignored while metadata reflects request
pass - invalid def_code rejected with enumerated error
pass - pagination limit must be integer >= 1
pass - extraneous filter key accepted without effect

mismatches: Docs describe Result.id as a string, but live responses return integers (e.g., 6310).
Docs state pagination limit/page determine the slice of results, yet the service returns the full dataset regardless of those values.
Docs list the default sort as 'id', while observed default ordering is by 'code' descending.
Documentation highlights only CARES-era DEFC codes, but validation accepts a broader enumerated list (e.g., '1'-'9', 'AAA', 'QQQ').
gaps: Impact of 'spending_type' remains unverified because all tested values returned identical data.
Unclear which shared filters beyond 'def_codes' are honored; time period inputs had no observable effect.
Unknown whether the service ever enforces actual pagination slicing when result sets exceed observed counts.
risks: Clients may assume metadata-driven pagination works and inadvertently refetch the complete dataset each request.
Silently ignored or malformed filters can mislead callers into believing results are constrained when they are not.
Large monetary values and award counts require handling beyond 32-bit integer precision.
Extraneous keys accepted inside 'filter' can conceal misconfigured integrations.
